mnemonics: NESTed Sets: A sequence of Nested, non-Empty, closed Sets whose diameters Tend to zero has a unique intersection point.
keyPoints: The sets must be nested (each set is a subset of the previous one).
The diameters of the sets must converge to zero.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the sets need to be closed (open sets can have empty intersection even if nested and shrinking).
Assuming the theorem holds if the diameter condition is not met (the intersection may be empty or contain more than one point).
